provisions.chapter: CHAPTER 00:00 SERIOUS CRIMES OF INTERNATIONAL CONCERN ACT
provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Serious Crimes of International Concern Act.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER 00:00 SERIOUS CRIMES OF INTERNATIONAL CONCERN ACT
provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content:
2. Interpretation
In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires—
"conventional international law" means a convention, treaty or other international agreement to which Botswana is a party and for the time being in force;
"Geneva Conventions" means the four Geneva Conventions adopted on 12 August 1949, the additional protocols to those Conventions and any future amendments to the Conventions; and
"property" has the meaning assigned to it under the Proceeds and Instruments of Crime Act.

definitions.definition: a convention, treaty or other international agreement to which Botswana is a party and for the time being in force
the four Geneva Conventions adopted on 12 August 1949, the additional protocols to those Conventions and any future amendments to the Conventions
assigned to it under the Proceeds and Instruments of Crime Act.
